summary: Estonia operates a comprehensive e-Health system where patients can view their complete medical records online through the Patient Portal (digilugu.ee). The system aggregates data from all healthcare providers into a unified national health record, including diagnoses, prescriptions, lab results, imaging, and vaccination history. Healthcare providers access records through the Health Information System (TIS), which exchanges data securely via X-Road infrastructure.
notes: Estonia's e-Health system has been operational since 2008. 99% of health data is digitized, and e-Prescriptions account for 99% of all prescriptions issued. The system uses blockchain technology (KSI) to ensure data integrity. Patients can control access to their records and see which providers have accessed their data.
